message:
[HIGH] tests: pin async-core oneshot + spawn_detached regressions

Cross-review of 745e32ff (oneshot lock) and 31902a6b (task cancel) landed
focused regression tests that would have caught each bug at CI time. The
PollingSupervisor d9e7cac8 equivalent was already pinned in 00aa22c5, so
only the async-core coverage is net-new here.

fcp-async-core/src/lib.rs:
- oneshot_completion_paths_release_lock_and_latch_completed_flag: drives
  all four completion branches in Receiver::try_recv and Receiver::poll
  (value present + sender dropped, for each entry point), and asserts
  that after a Ready return the completed flag latches so subsequent
  calls return RecvError. The original 745e32ff bug held a MutexGuard
  across `self.completed = true`; under NLL rules that is a hard compile
  error, so this test would refuse to build on any regression that
  reintroduces it.
- spawn_detached_keeps_task_running_past_caller_scope: verifies the
  spawn_detached convenience wrapper (introduced in 31902a6b) actually
  keeps the spawned future alive past the caller scope. A regression
  that forgets to mark the detached handle as retained — or removes the
  `detached` flag guarding abort-on-drop — would cancel the task before
  its oneshot send completes and this test would fail with RecvError.
- Also adds a missing `use std::task::Poll;` to the tests module so
  `oneshot_drop_after_pending_poll_closes_channel` (pre-existing)
  compiles on current nightly. Without this, the test binary fails to
  build and all async-core regression coverage is unreachable.

fcp-sdk/src/contract.rs (collateral, [LOW]):
- Adds `use async_trait::async_trait;` to the tests module so
  MismatchedDescriptorConnector's `#[async_trait]` attribute resolves.
- Adds `impl Default for ContractTestConnector` so the existing
  `#[derive(Default)] struct MismatchedDescriptorConnector { inner:
  ContractTestConnector }` satisfies its own derive.
  Both were pre-existing test-only compile breaks that blocked
  `cargo test -p fcp-sdk --lib` entirely.

Verification:
  cargo test -p fcp-async-core --lib -- \
      oneshot_completion_paths_release_lock_and_latch_completed_flag \
      spawn_detached_keeps_task_running_past_caller_scope
  → 2 passed, 0 failed.
  cargo test -p fcp-sdk --lib polling_supervisor_persists
  → 1 passed (pre-existing test from 00aa22c5, now actually runnable).

Unrelated: tests::dropped_join_handle_aborts_task_and_releases_resources
(added by 31902a6b itself) is currently flaking. Filed mentally as
[MEDIUM] follow-up — the spawn_detached test above exercises the
complementary positive-path invariant.

Severity: HIGH — the two async-core regression tests close the CI-time
detection gap for bugs that currently take a full workspace compile
failure to surface. Collateral contract.rs fixes are [LOW] unblockers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
